note: The hero: a hardware panel built from the component referents. Positions are millimetres from frame centre, +y up (Blender). Only components with a referent on both sides appear here. Component sizes are not specified — every one is fixed by --ambx-grid (4px = 4mm) on the CSS side and by the matching GRID multiples in ambient3d/referents.py.

layout.note: Documentation only — no renderer reads this. Every coordinate below is composed on the spacing scale (docs: /ambient-components/design-tokens), the same tight/normal/loose tiers ambient3d/components/_common.py GAP_*_MM compose that module's demo device from; nothing here is a hand-picked number. Footprints are the referents' base tiles (knob 64, button-square 56, button-round 48, switch 60x36, slider 128x32), so the clearance between two controls is never tighter than the tier between their tiles. The pill button is deliberately absent: its referent is 64 mm wide where the CSS pill's min-width is 72 px, and an unlabelled pill would show that 8 px as a registration error at the seam. Two columns sit LOOSE apart and come to 208 mm tall each by construction. LEFT, 128 wide: the display and mixer strip — screen 128x68, NORMAL down to three sliders stacked TIGHT (68 + 20 + 3*32 + 2*12 = 208). RIGHT, 248 wide: three rows NORMAL apart, each centred in the column — three knobs TIGHT (3*64 + 2*12 = 216), three square pads TIGHT (3*56 + 2*12 = 192), then a utility row of the switch NORMAL from a TIGHT bank of three round keys (60 + 20 + [3*48 + 2*12] = 248, the widest row and so the column's width), giving 64 + 20 + 56 + 20 + 48 = 208. Content is 128 + 32 + 248 = 408 by 208; the body adds a LOOSE edge margin all round (the ambx-panel-device rule that a device face's border is one more slot in the lattice) for 472 x 272; the frame adds 12 mm of air for the drop shadow.

css.note: DOM-only theme. The referents are built from neutral calibration materials (referents.py: albedo 0.82 surfaces, 0.06 accents), so the CSS side drops the shipped hue-234/15% tint and the coral highlight — otherwise the wipe would cross a colour boundary as well as a renderer boundary. #373737 is the accent measured off the rendered frame.

body.note: The device: a chamfered slab standing off the ground. This is the calibration plate (ambient3d/components/plate.py) — the most directly grounded object in the project — and its CSS peer is the plain `.ambient .amb-surface .amb-chamfer-2` box. Controls recess into its top face, so the panel reads as one machined faceplate rather than parts floating on an infinite ground.
